guidance.whenToUse: Use the Accordion component to display multiple sections of related content in a confined space, allowing users to expand and collapse sections to reveal or hide information. It's particularly useful for FAQs, detailed product specifications, nested navigation menus, or any scenario where content needs to be organized without overwhelming the user initially.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use Accordion for critical information that must always be immediately visible. Avoid using it when users need to compare content across different sections simultaneously, as only one or a few sections are typically visible at a time. If there is only a single piece of content to show/hide, a simpler Collapsible component or a Button with a disclosure pattern would be more appropriate.
guidance.accessibility: The Accordion component inherits strong accessibility features from Radix UI. It includes proper ARIA roles and attributes (e.g., `role="heading"`, `aria-controls`, `aria-expanded`) for assistive technologies. Keyboard navigation is fully supported, allowing users to navigate between accordion triggers using Tab and expand/collapse sections using Enter or Space keys.

common.description: An accordion with multiple items where only one item can be open at a time. Expanding one item automatically collapses the previously open one.

common.title: Accordion with Multiple Items (Single Open)

common.description: An accordion configured to allow multiple items to be open simultaneously. Each item can be expanded and collapsed independently.

<Accordion.Root type="single" collapsible><Accordion.Item value="item-1"><Accordion.Header><Accordion.Trigger>Enabled Item</Accordion.Trigger></Accordion.Header><Accordion.Content>This item is enabled.</Accordion.Content></Accordion.Item><Accordion.Item value="item-2" disabled><Accordion.Header><Accordion.Trigger>Disabled Item</Accordion.Trigger></Accordion.Header><Accordion.Content>This item is disabled and cannot be opened.</Accordion.Content></Accordion.Item></Accordion.Root>

common.description: Demonstrates an accordion where one of the items is disabled, preventing it from being expanded or collapsed by user interaction.

minimal.title: Basic Accordion
minimal.code: <Accordion.Root type="single" collapsible><Accordion.Item value="item-1"><Accordion.Header><Accordion.Trigger>Is it accessible?</Accordion.Trigger></Accordion.Header><Accordion.Content>Yes. It adheres to WAI-ARIA design patterns.</Accordion.Content></Accordion.Item></Accordion.Root>

response.description: The Accordion component is a user interface element designed to display a list of collapsible panels. Each panel can hide or show its content, allowing users to progressively disclose information. It is built on top of Radix UI's Accordion primitive, providing robust accessibility features and flexible customization. This component is ideal for organizing large amounts of content into digestible sections, reducing visual clutter and improving content scannability. It supports both single-item and multiple-item expansion modes, making it versatile for various use cases such as FAQs, detailed product descriptions, or navigation menus.
